question_title: What will be the value of "Nominal Broad U.S. Dollar Index" on 2026-03-12?
summary: The Nominal Broad US Dollar Index (DTWEXBGS) has been in a remarkably stable regime since January 26, 2026, trading around 117.85 with daily std of 0.34. The last FRED data point is 117.9917 on Feb 20. DXY futures data through March 2 shows essentially no net movement since then (+0.07%), suggesting DTWEXBGS remains near 118.0. Conflicting forces include mild upward pressure from Iran conflict safe-haven flows and mild downward pressure from structural dollar weakness and the Supreme Court striking down IEEPA tariffs. Monte Carlo simulation using regime-specific volatility (0.276%/day) over 8 trading days from ~118.05 produces a symmetric distribution centered at 118.05 with P10-P90 range of 116.87-119.23.

factors.description: Stable regime since Jan 26: DTWEXBGS has traded in tight 117.44-118.50 range with mean 117.85 and std 0.34 over 19 observations. Strong mean-reversion tendency within regime.
DXY essentially flat from Feb 20 through Mar 1 (97.80 to 97.79), suggesting DTWEXBGS near last known value of ~118.0. DX=F futures show backwardation (Mar 97.86, Jun 97.57).
Iran conflict (US/Israel strike, death of Khamenei) pushed DXY up ~0.5% on Mar 2 - safe-haven dollar bid. Geopolitical risk could sustain elevated dollar.
Supreme Court struck down Trump IEEPA tariffs - removes tariff-related trade uncertainty, mixed dollar impact (could reduce defensive dollar positioning OR reduce drag on US economy).
factors.conditional: If tariff removal reduces dollar demand

factors.description: Broader 2025-2026 structural trend: USD declining from ~130 to ~118, driven by Fed rate cuts, weakening safe-haven status, and trade policy uncertainty.
Monte Carlo simulation: 500K paths, 8 trading days forward from 118.05, regime vol 0.276%/day, zero drift. Median 118.05, std 0.92.

reasoning:
**Forecast: DTWEXBGS on 2026-03-12**

| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| P10 | 116.87 |
| P20 | 117.27 |
| P40 | 117.81 |
| P60 | 118.28 |
| P80 | 118.82 |
| P90 | 119.23 |

**Key reasoning:**
- The index has been in a stable regime since Jan 26, centered around 117.85 with low daily volatility (0.28%)
- DXY data through March 2 confirms essentially no movement since the last FRED reading of 117.99 on Feb 20
- Conflicting forces: Iran safe-haven bid (+) vs. structural dollar weakness and IEEPA tariff ruling (-)
- The 8-day horizon and low regime volatility produce a tight, symmetric distribution centered at ~118.05
